title: Day 1 Forecast Discussion
discussion:
CESTOF SEVERE WEATHER DISCUSSION
Issued for: 2nd May 2026
Area: Belgium, North France, West Germany, Switzerland, North Spain, North Portugal

HIGHEST RISK OF THE DAY: Slight Risk

The highest risk area includes: Belgium, North France, South East Netherlands, West Germany.

A Slight Risk means a few storms may become severe, especially where the best ingredients overlap.

RISK AREAS:
France, Germany, Belgium, Netherlands, Spain, Switzerland, Portugal

METEOROLOGICAL DISCUSSION:

A severe-weather forecast has been issued for parts of Belgium, North France, West Germany, Switzerland, North Spain, North Portugal, mainly during Evening to Night, as several convective ingredients are expected to overlap. The primary forcing mechanism should be a convergence zone may focus storm initiation where surface heating is strongest. In addition, forcing is moderate, supporting scattered initiation where instability is strongest, while a convergence line may focus initiation and locally enhance storm organization. Early-day evolution may also play an important role, as clearer skies during the morning may allow stronger surface heating and destabilization.

Thermodynamically, dew points are sufficient for stronger convection and moderate instability, and moisture depth is high, increasing the risk of heavy rainfall in stronger or slow-moving storms. At the same time, moderate instability is present, with CAPE around 1000–2000 J/kg, while mid-level lapse rates are moderate, supporting some stronger updrafts. This combination will determine how strong updrafts can become and whether storms are capable of producing hail or damaging wind gusts. Cloud bases also matter, as LCL heights are moderate, allowing some low-level rotation potential in stronger storms. Meanwhile, some CIN is present, meaning initiation may depend on stronger local forcing or surface heating, meaning the final threat may depend strongly on whether storms can initiate in the best environment.

Kinematically, deep-layer shear is very strong, above 35 m/s, supporting supercells or organized storm systems if storms remain discrete. Near the surface, low-level shear is strong, which may increase tornado potential near favorable boundaries, and strong low-level helicity is present, making tornado potential more relevant if surface-based supercells develop. Furthermore, upper-level support is weak, which may limit large-scale ascent and storm ventilation. These wind-field ingredients will control storm organization, rotation potential and the chance for storms to become long-lived.

Storm coverage is expected to be numerous, meaning numerous storms may develop, increasing the overall severe-weather coverage. In terms of movement, storm motion should be moderate, allowing storms to produce hazards without staying over one place for too long. Storms may become supercellular, especially where cells remain discrete. In that case, large hail and possible tornadoes would become more relevant. Because of this, the final severe-weather threat will depend on how storms initiate and evolve. If storms remain discrete for a longer time, hail and rotation may become more relevant. If storms merge quickly into clusters or lines, damaging wind gusts and heavy rainfall will likely become the dominant hazards.

SETUP TYPE:

The setup is mixed, with some ingredients supporting stronger storms but uncertainty remaining in coverage, mode or intensity.

MAIN HAZARDS:

The main hazards are expected to be a tornado risk and frequent lightning. The most important hazard signals are a low tornado threat and possible discrete supercells.

LIMITING FACTORS:

No major limiting factors were selected.

FORECAST REASONING:

The forecast was issued because the environment contains enough ingredients for thunderstorms to become stronger or organized. Instability will provide energy for updrafts, while vertical wind shear will determine how organized and long-lived storms can become.

Where the best CAPE overlaps with stronger deep-layer shear, forcing and boundaries, storms may become more intense. If storms remain isolated, the threat for hail and possible rotation may be higher. If storms merge quickly into clusters or lines, the main threat will likely shift toward damaging wind gusts and heavy rainfall.

The final severity will depend on storm initiation. If forcing is strong enough and CIN is overcome, storms may develop rapidly. However, if cloud cover, weak lift or stronger inhibition remains in place, the overall threat may stay lower than the environment suggests.

CONFIDENCE:

Forecast confidence is high because the main ingredients overlap well and storm initiation appears more likely.

SUMMARY:

Overall, Slight Risk is the highest risk for this forecast. The most important area is Belgium, North France, South East Netherlands, West Germany. Severe weather is not expected everywhere, but storms that form in the best environment may become strong to severe. The main threats are a tornado risk and frequent lightning.
